original_reasoning: Batnae is Aramaic/Syrian place. btn=interior/belly/hidden valley. Shared Semitic substrate plausible.
Arabic خشع can denote a low, flattened, adhering form; Greek gape/open wide may preserve the opposite side of a terrain-opening concept.
Arabic aged old man or old woman can drift toward grey as an age-marked colour concept.
Both point to a contemptible human type; insult and babbler are a plausible pejorative chain.

Arabic jurthuma = collected earth at tree roots, accumulation, cluster. Greek ageraimetha = 1pl aor middle optative of ageiro 'we should gather/collect'. Both center on gathering/accumulation. Strong direct link.
Karmad (brick, tile-like construction) vs 'small tile' - clear tile/brick link
Arabic qakh = 'insides rotted/spoiled by disease'; Greek = 'sick, wasted body'. Plausible semantic link via disease-rotting body.
Gaza from Semitic 3zz=strong. Ja'iz=permitted is different root. Phonetic coincidence, possible substrate.
